name: Joe Mooney
description: American jazz and pop accordionist and organist (1911–1975)
abstract: Joe Mooney (March 14, 1911 – May 12, 1975) was an American jazz and pop accordionist, organist, and vocalist.

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: You know I think he did it that way because he saw Joe Mooney do it that way... And that’s when we learned tunes he would just start to play it and I’d go like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, October 18, 2023 [interview 2 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Rudy Van Gelder
about_quotes.evidence: The first one that I ever did, that was with Bucky Pizzarelli, a player by the name of Joe Mooney, and a bass player... He was very…I made the trio
about_quotes.doc_title: Rudy Van Gelder — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Bucky Pizzarelli
about_quotes.evidence: he heard Nat King Cole and Joe Mooney from Paterson, New Jersey, who is the father of all the right chords for my money.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bucky Pizzarelli interviewed by Monk Rowe, Aspen, Colorado, October 11, 1997 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: John Pizzarelli, Jr.
about_quotes.evidence: my father had some old scratchy Joe Mooney records so I did “Man with a Million Dollars,” and “Have Another One Not Me,”
about_quotes.doc_title: John Pizzarelli, Jr. interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 29, 2000 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Abe Most
about_quotes.evidence: there was Joe Mooney, I don’t know if you remember that name, he used to sing and play accordion.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abe Most interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, February 13, 1999

about_quotes.speaker: Sonny Igoe
about_quotes.evidence: I had had the good fortune to have met a fellow named Joe Mooney
about_quotes.doc_title: Sonny Igoe interviewed by Monk Rowe, Emerson, New Jersey, July 31, 2003
